fix: guard getDatabase with promise mutex to prevent workout detail crash (BLD-3)

The singleton pattern in getDatabase() had no concurrency guard. When
multiple components called getDatabase() concurrently during startup,
the database handle was returned before migration completed, causing
NativeDatabase.prepareAsync NullPointerException on Android.

The fix uses a shared init promise so only one initialization runs and
all concurrent callers wait for it to complete. On failure, the promise
resets to allow retry.

Fixes #14

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: wait for Blazor circuit before form interaction in tests

The WebFormsForm Playwright tests were racing against Blazor Server
circuit establishment. During prerendering, the form renders without
@onsubmit:preventDefault, so clicking submit before the circuit is
ready causes a full-page POST that resets component state.

Changes:
- Add data-interactive attribute to WebFormsForm when in interactive mode
- Wait for form[data-interactive] in tests before filling/submitting
- Tighten input locators to match actual field names (username, email)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use OnAfterRender for interactive detection in WebFormsForm

HttpContextAccessor.HttpContext is never null in Blazor Server (even
during SignalR circuits, it returns the connection's HTTP context).
Replace with OnAfterRender-based detection which only fires on
interactive circuits, never during prerendering or static SSR.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: return interop form data over HttpContext in RequestShim.Form

In Blazor Server interactive circuits, HttpContext is available (from
the SignalR WebSocket connection), but its Request.Form contains the
WebSocket upgrade request data, not the interactive form submission.
When SetFormData() has been called (WebFormsForm submit), the cached
interop-backed FormShim must take priority.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix stale page on reload: switch index.html to network-first SW strategy

The service worker was using cache-first for index.html, meaning any
update to the page code was not visible until the new SW installed and
activated — which required all tabs to close first. This caused
intermittent missing appName/icon rendering (and other stale-code issues)
because the old cached page was served while fresh API data was returned.

Changes:
- index.html / '/' now use network-first (network failure falls back to cache)
- Truly static assets (manifest, favicon, qrcode.min.js) keep cache-first
- API and all other requests bypass the SW cache entirely
- Added skipWaiting() + clients.claim() so new SW activates immediately
  without waiting for all tabs to close
- Bumped cache name to web-notifications-v5
- Updated copilot-instructions.md to reflect new strategy

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- updateStats: coalesce via rAF instead of updating DOM every message

- Reduce MAX_LOG from 500 to 200

commit.message:
fix: apply pending transfers at 8am deadline, add auto-application (#50)

* fix: apply pending transfers at 8am deadline, add auto-application

GW2 transfers to not apply:

1. applyPendingChanges() compared against midnight Saturday instead of the
   8am transfer deadline. Transfers submitted between midnight and 8am
   Saturday were treated as current-week, deferring them to GW3 instead
   of applying for GW2.

2. applyPendingChanges() was only called from the /my-team endpoint.
   Leaderboards, profiles, and team comparisons all read the old team
   until the user manually visited My Team.

Fixes:
- Fix deadline comparison to use TEAM_ELIGIBILITY_HOUR (8am)
- Add applyAllPendingChanges() for bulk application
- Add scheduled-apply-transfers (cron: 8am every Saturday)
- Add admin-apply-transfers endpoint for manual intervention
- Add safety-net applyPendingChanges calls to leaderboard-periods,
  user-profile, and user-team-compare endpoints

commit.message:
fix: remove @secure() from appInsightsConnectionString Bicep output

Azure ARM omits the value field from secure outputs in the deployment
API response. The deploy.ps1 script accesses \.appInsightsConnectionString.value
after parsing the deployment JSON, causing a PropertyNotFoundStrict error
under Set-StrictMode -Version Latest.

Application Insights connection strings are not true secrets — they are
routinely embedded in app configs and client-side telemetry code — so
@secure() is not appropriate here.

index-based assignment would silently map the wrong embedding to the
wrong ingredient. Wrap both the primary and alias embedding-assignment
loops in an else branch so they are skipped entirely on length mismatch,
leaving affected items unmatched rather than potentially mis-matched.
